title: barjonia furlanii
intro: Barjonia furlanii, also known as the Furlani's Trumpet Vine or Queen of the Night, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is a tropical plant native to Madagascar, known for its showy, fragrant flowers that bloom at night and wilt before dawn. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Barjonia furlanii include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, and it is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses.
